title: Stylized-Fact Preserving Financial Diffusion with Distributional Risk Controls
objective: Test whether stylized-fact losses and distributional risk controls improve financial diffusion models for realistic, auditable stress-scenario generation.

datasets.name: S&P 500 / Yahoo Finance OHLCV
preprocessing: Compute log returns from adjusted close prices.
Create fixed-length rolling windows, e.g. 64 or 128 trading days.
Split chronologically into train, validation, and held-out test regimes.
Normalize using training-period statistics only to avoid leakage.

datasets.name: Crisis/regime slices
datasets.purpose: Check whether controls remain faithful outside calm market conditions.

models.proposed: Conditional financial diffusion with stylized-fact losses and risk-control conditioning.
ablations: Diffusion with individual stylized-fact losses removed one at a time
baselines: Sig-Wasserstein GAN as the closest path-signature baseline
CoFinDiff as the closest controllable financial diffusion baseline if reproducible

hypothesisTests.claim: Stylized-fact losses improve preservation of heavy tails, volatility clustering, and cross-asset dependence.
hypothesisTests.independentVariable: Training objective: diffusion with vs. without stylized-fact losses.
primaryMetrics: Tail-index error using Hill estimator or comparable tail estimator
Absolute-return autocorrelation error across multiple lags
Correlation-matrix distance, e.g. Frobenius distance or eigenvalue-spectrum error
hypothesisTests.successCriterion: Proposed model significantly reduces primary stylized-fact errors versus the ablated diffusion and is competitive with or better than GAN baselines.
hypothesisTests.statisticalCheck: Bootstrap test windows and report confidence intervals for metric differences.

hypothesisTests.claim: Risk controls produce monotonic and calibrated changes in generated volatility, VaR/ES, and drawdown.
hypothesisTests.independentVariable: Requested control level, e.g. low/medium/high volatility or target drawdown quantile.
primaryMetrics: Spearman correlation between requested and realized volatility
Calibration error for VaR and expected shortfall targets
secondaryMetrics: Failure rate for infeasible or artifact-like paths
hypothesisTests.successCriterion: Realized risk metrics are monotonic in requested control levels and calibrated within pre-specified tolerance bands on held-out periods.
hypothesisTests.statisticalCheck: Use monotonic trend tests plus bootstrap confidence intervals over generated samples and test windows.

hypothesisTests.claim: Signature-based evaluation reveals path-level failures missed by marginal metrics.
hypothesisTests.independentVariable: Evaluation metric family: marginal distribution metrics vs. path/signature metrics.
primaryMetrics: Sig-Wasserstein distance between real and generated path distributions
Rank disagreement between marginal-only and path-level evaluations
Detection rate on synthetic counterexamples with matched marginals but broken temporal ordering
secondaryMetrics: Stress-test ranking agreement with historical regimes
hypothesisTests.successCriterion: Signature metrics identify temporal-dependence failures in cases where marginal metrics remain similar.
hypothesisTests.statisticalCheck: Construct controlled perturbations and compare metric sensitivity across repeated samples.

mainExperiment.setup: Train all models on identical chronological training windows and evaluate on held-out windows plus stress-regime slices.
mainExperiment.comparison: Proposed model vs. baselines and ablations on both realism and controllability.
mainExperiment.targetOutcome: Lower stylized-fact and path-level errors than ablated diffusion, comparable or better realism than strong baselines, and uniquely monotonic calibrated risk controls.

sanityChecks: Compare against historical bootstrap/resampling to ensure the generator beats simple empirical baselines.
Verify no train-test leakage from normalization, constituent selection, or rolling-window construction.
Inspect generated OHLCV-derived paths for impossible prices, extreme arbitrage artifacts, or degenerate volatility collapse.
Run multiple random seeds and report mean plus confidence intervals.
expectedRisks: CoFinDiff may not have a reproducible implementation; use published protocol or mark as partial baseline.
Tail-index estimates can be noisy; report sensitivity to threshold choice.
Controls may trade off realism against calibration; report Pareto curves rather than a single scalar only.
